changes.v1_summary: Definitions included API, Booking, Consumed Booking, Net Rate and Confidential Information.
changes.v2_summary: Adds a Business Day definition. Cosmetic — clarifies terminology without shifting obligations.
changes.agent_test_notes: Correct behavior: detect textual change but classify as cosmetic. No routing, no escalation.

changes.v1_summary: Net Rate defined as the supplier rate before Nexus-added amounts unless expressly included.
changes.v2_summary: Net Rate rephrased from 'before the addition of' to 'exclusive of'. Legal-equivalent under English law.
changes.agent_test_notes: Correct behavior: cosmetic rewording. Likely failure mode: treating 'exclusive of' vs 'before the addition of' as a pricing scope change.

changes.v1_summary: Nexus retained unilateral right to amend commission rate with 30 days written notice.
changes.v2_summary: Commission amendments now require mutual written agreement with 60 days notice. Removes Nexus unilateral right.
changes.compound_risk_description: Commission control lost (C3) + credit halved (C4) + StayLink can reduce credit further on volume trigger (C5) = margin compression risk. No single clause creates this — the combination removes Nexus's commercial floor.
changes.agent_test_notes: Important: this change is FAVORABLE to Nexus — it removes the partner's unilateral power. Must NOT be flagged as a policy violation. Likely failure mode: agent flags it as a violation of commission control when it actually improves Nexus's position.

changes.v1_summary: Nexus operated under a €50,000 bi-weekly credit facility.
changes.policy_rule_violated: POL-007: credit facility reduction requires Finance sign-off.
changes.compound_risk_description: Part of three-clause margin compression risk. Static credit reduction (C4) combined with commission control loss (C3) and dynamic downward trigger (C5) removes Nexus's financial floor.
changes.agent_test_notes: Correct behavior: flag credit reduction, route to Finance. Likely failure mode: missing the 50% reduction magnitude.

changes.v1_summary: No conditional credit reduction mechanism existed.
changes.v2_summary: StayLink gains unilateral right to reduce credit facility if Nexus booking volume falls below €10,000/month.
changes.policy_rule_violated: POL-001: partner unilateral modification not permitted. POL-007: credit contingency requires Finance sign-off.
changes.compound_risk_description: Amplifying clause in the margin compression risk. Adds a dynamic volume-triggered downgrade on top of the static reduction in C4, while C3 removes Nexus's ability to offset via commission.
changes.agent_test_notes: Correct behavior: flag both the unilateral trigger and Finance sign-off requirement. Likely failure mode: missing the dynamic downgrade mechanism, only catching the static reduction in C4.

changes.v1_summary: StayLink required to use commercially reasonable efforts for competitive rates.
changes.v2_summary: Changed to reasonable endeavours. Legal-equivalent under English law in this context.
changes.agent_test_notes: Correct behavior: cosmetic under English law. Likely failure mode: over-triggering on the effort standard change.

changes.v1_summary: P1 incident resolution within 4 hours. Nexus termination right after 3 P1 incidents in 4 months.
changes.v2_summary: P1 resolution extended to 8 hours. Termination trigger unchanged.
changes.policy_rule_violated: POL-006: P1 resolution time increase is material and requires Product/Tech review.
changes.compound_risk_description: Slower P1 response combined with C11's termination friction creates compounded operational exposure — Nexus can't exit quickly and must endure longer outages.
changes.agent_test_notes: Correct behavior: material SLA degradation, route to Product/Tech primary. Must tag compound risk with C11. Likely failure mode: routing to CS instead of Product/Tech, or missing the compound risk link.

changes.v1_summary: Mutual liability cap at 12 months fees, no Nexus-specific carve-outs.
changes.v2_summary: Adds carve-out: liability cap does not apply to claims from Nexus's failure to implement StayLink's required API security updates.
changes.policy_rule_violated: POL-003: asymmetric carve-out applied exclusively to Nexus requires Legal sign-off.
changes.agent_test_notes: Correct behavior: flag asymmetric carve-out, route Legal primary. Likely failure mode: reading as narrow cybersecurity clause and missing that StayLink controls the trigger via its own documentation.

changes.v1_summary: StayLink processed Nexus traveler data as data processor under a separate DPA.
changes.v2_summary: Processor relationship and DPA requirement removed. Both parties now independent controllers.
changes.policy_rule_violated: POL-005: removal of DPA requirement requires Legal sign-off.
changes.agent_test_notes: Correct behavior: treat as major data allocation change, not drafting cleanup. Flag and escalate to Legal. Likely failure mode: both versions mention independent controllers so agent misses that processor role for traveler data was removed.

changes.v1_summary: Confidentiality exceptions were a closed list.
changes.v2_summary: Adds 'including but not limited to' making exceptions non-exhaustive. Subtly broadens what falls outside confidentiality obligations.
changes.agent_test_notes: Borderline material — defensible either way. Classified material because broadening exception scope is a real shift even if low severity. No routing required.

changes.v1_summary: Nexus could exercise SLA termination rights directly.
changes.v2_summary: Nexus must now provide StayLink a written remediation plan and 30-day cure period before exercising SLA termination rights.
changes.policy_rule_violated: POL-008: Nexus termination right for SLA breach must not be conditioned on providing a remediation plan to the partner.
changes.compound_risk_description: Combined with C7's P1 SLA extension, Nexus faces both longer outages and harder exit — compounded operational exposure.
changes.agent_test_notes: Correct behavior: flag as policy violation and link to C7 compound risk. Likely failure mode: treating as generic legal process language without connecting to SLA deterioration.

changes.v1_summary: Agreement governed by laws of England and Wales.
changes.v2_summary: Agreement now governed by laws of Spain.
changes.policy_rule_violated: POL-004: governing law change requires Legal sign-off. Spain is partner's local law — acceptable per policy but not automatic.
changes.agent_test_notes: Near-miss not hard violation — Spain is StayLink's local law which is acceptable per playbook. But still requires Legal sign-off. Likely failure mode: treating as boilerplate or escalating to Leadership before Legal.

changes.v1_summary: Exclusive jurisdiction in courts of England.
changes.v2_summary: Exclusive jurisdiction now in courts of Barcelona, Spain.
changes.policy_rule_violated: POL-004: jurisdiction change requires Legal sign-off.
changes.agent_test_notes: Separate entry from C12 — governing law and jurisdiction are distinct changes even though both in clause 15. Likely failure mode: merging with C12 and only producing one flag.

changes.compound_risk_description: Uncapped liability carve-out (C8) + DPA removal (C9) = data processing responsibilities ambiguous precisely when uncapped liability exposure is highest.
changes.agent_test_notes: CR3 was surfaced by the model unprompted in Session 4. Validated as genuine compound risk. Bonus find — if model misses it in a given run, document as known variance.
